concept: Sustainability / The Path of Perpetual Balance
description: The practice of creating systems that can continue indefinitely without depleting resources or causing harm, ensuring future abundance by meeting present needs wisely.
rationale: The grounded 'na' roots us in present reality - sustainability begins with honest assessment of what is. This grounding connects through 'lo', the thread linking now to then, this generation to those unborn. The open 'a' ensures space for future flourishing, refusing to close doors our descendants need. Three syllables span time: grounding in now, connecting through time, opening to forever - the wisdom of taking only what can be given back.
